skill.name: specdd-review
skill.description: Review code changes against active SpecDD specifications and report contract violations without editing files.
skill.summary: Checks diffs against governing SpecDD contracts and reports review findings.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static detections for shell execution and weak cryptography are false positives caused by markdown backticks and ordinary frontmatter text in SKILL.md. The skill has no executable code, network behavior, secret access, or prompt injection text, but it intentionally asks the agent to read local SpecDD files for review context.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged locations contain inline code formatting for file paths and SpecDD terms, not executable shell commands or Ruby backtick execution. No command invocation, dynamic input execution, or script body is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact flagged lines are markdown prose and list items, with backticks used only to format file names and terms. There is no executable language context or command-dispatch API.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Detection Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged frontmatter description line contains a plain English description of the skill. It does not reference a cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, cipher, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata text describing when to use the skill. No cryptographic code or algorithm name appears at the cited location.

low_findings.title: Scoped Local File Reading Is Required For Operation
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read SpecDD bootstrap files, referenced specs, and governing contracts in the workspace. This is legitimate for a review skill, but users should expect local project files to be read during review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require reading local SpecDD files to establish the active contract chain. The behavior is narrow and aligned with the stated review purpose.

content.user_title: Review SpecDD Changes Against Active Specs
content.value_statement: SpecDD projects need reviews that compare changes to the active specification chain. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to find contract violations and residual risk.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the active SpecDD bootstrap chain before review.
Resolves governing specifications for target files, paths, or behaviors.
Compares diffs against Must, Must not, Scenario, Tasks, and Done when entries.
Reports findings with severity, violated contract, behavior risk, and likely fix.
Calls out underspecified behavior as uncertainty instead of inventing requirements.
Keeps review summaries brief and places findings first.
limitations: Only applies to projects that already contain a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.
Does not edit files unless the user explicitly requests changes.
Requires readable SpecDD files and enough diff context to evaluate behavior.
Does not replace project tests or human judgment for ambiguous contracts.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests Against Specs
use_cases.description: Compare a proposed change with the active SpecDD contracts and report concrete violations before merge.

use_cases.title: Check Feature Work Before Handoff
use_cases.description: Confirm that implementation work still matches governing scenarios, tasks, and done criteria.

use_cases.title: Audit Contract Coverage
use_cases.description: Find places where changed behavior is underspecified or where verification does not match the risk.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Current Diff
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-review to review my current diff against the active SpecDD specs. Lead with findings and include residual risk.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of local uncommitted changes

prompt_templates.title: Review Specific Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-review for these changed files: [file list]. Resolve the governing spec chain and report any contract violations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review for a known target set

prompt_templates.title: Check Verification Strength
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-review to compare this change with the active specs and judge whether the verification matches the behavioral risk.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewing test coverage and acceptance evidence

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Ambiguous Spec Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-review on this change. Separate confirmed spec violations from underspecified behavior and open questions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review where requirements may be incomplete

output_examples.input: Review my current diff against SpecDD.
output_examples.output: Findings first, ordered by severity. Each finding names the affected file, violated SpecDD contract, behavior risk, and smallest likely fix.

output_examples.input: Check whether this feature is done according to the specs.
output_examples.output: A concise review that compares the change with Tasks and Done when entries, then lists missing verification or residual risk.

output_examples.input: Review these files and call out unclear requirements.
output_examples.output: Confirmed violations are separated from open questions. Underspecified behavior is reported as uncertainty rather than a false violation.

best_practices: Run the skill from the root of an existing SpecDD project.
Provide the changed files, diff, or behavior under review.
Ask for exact contract quotes when a finding depends on specific spec wording.
anti_patterns: Using the skill in a project without .specdd/bootstrap.md.
Asking it to invent requirements that are not present in governing specs.
Treating the review as a replacement for tests or maintainer approval.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews code changes against active SpecDD bootstrap files, governing specs, scenarios, tasks, and done criteria.

faq.question: Does it modify my code?
faq.answer: No. The skill says not to edit files unless the user explicitly asks for changes.

faq.question: Does it work outside SpecDD projects?
faq.answer: No. It is intended only for projects that already have a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.

faq.question: Can it use SpecDD CLI help?
faq.answer: Yes. When useful and available, it may consult the specdd-cli skill for governing-spec discovery.

faq.question: How does it handle unclear requirements?
faq.answer: It should report unclear behavior as an uncertainty or open question instead of inventing a violation.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
